message.content:
You are a senior frontend engineer reviewing a PR that adds a Kbd component to a Raycast UI clone project. 

The project uses: React 19, TypeScript strict, Sass (no CSS modules/tailwind), Bun runtime.

Review these two new files critically as a pull request reviewer. Check for:
1. Correctness and completeness against the spec
2. Consistency with existing component patterns in the project
3. Accessibility
4. Visual accuracy (does the styling match Raycast's keyboard shortcut badges?)
5. Edge cases
6. Any issues or improvements needed

SPEC from progress.txt:
"Build Kbd component (src/components/kbd/). Small inline element that renders a keyboard shortcut like '⌘K' or '↵'. Styled as subtle bordered pill/box matching Raycast's shortcut indicators. Background slightly lighter than surface. Border 1px solid $border. Border-radius $radius-sm. Padding 1px 4px. Font-size $font-xs. Color $text-secondary. Display inline-flex, gap 2px. Used inside ActionPanel and ListItem. Create kbd.tsx and kbd.scss."

Files to review:
- src/components/kbd/kbd.tsx
- src/components/kbd/kbd.scss

Also read these files for pattern consistency:
- src/components/action_panel/action_panel.tsx (uses shortcut ReactNode - Kbd will be used here)
- src/components/list/list_item/list_item.tsx (Kbd may be used as accessory)
- src/styles/_tokens.scss (design tokens)

Please read all these files and provide your review with specific, actionable feedback. Flag any issues as MUST-FIX or SUGGESTION.

content.text: I'll review the Kbd component PR by first reading all the relevant files to understand the implementation and context.
Now let me check if there are any other similar components in the project to understand patterns better, and look at how Raycast actually renders keyboard shortcuts.

content.text: Now let me research how Raycast actually renders keyboard shortcuts to ensure visual accuracy.

input.query: Raycast keyboard shortcut badge styling visual design 2026

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Raycast keyboard shortcut badge styling visual design 2026"

Links: [{"title":"Raycast - Your shortcut to everything","url":"https://www.raycast.com/"},{"title":"Design Tools for Mac | Raycast Design Extensions","url":"https://www.raycast.com/store/category/design-tools"},{"title":"A fresh look and feel - Raycast Blog","url":"https://www.raycast.com/blog/a-fresh-look-and-feel"},{"title":"Keyboard Shortcuts","url":"https://manual.raycast.com/keyboard-shortcuts"},{"title":"Raycast","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/keyboard.md"},{"title":"Keyboard | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/keyboard"},{"title":"Raycast | Dribbble","url":"https://dribbble.com/raycastapp"},{"title":"Raycast Store: Keyboard Shortcut Sequences","url":"https://www.raycast.com/HelloImSteven/keyboard-shortcut-sequences"},{"title":"Raycast for Designers: Your Command Center for Mac Workflows | Hack Design","url":"https://www.hackdesign.org/toolkit/raycast/"},{"title":"Command Aliases and Hotkeys","url":"https://manual.raycast.com/command-aliases-and-hotkeys"}]

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about Raycast's keyboard shortcut visual design:

The action bar in Raycast's interface shows actions available in the current context, along with their keyboard shortcut, which lets users learn action shortcuts to make using Raycast even faster.

Regarding the visual design approach, Raycast's design team chose a keycap as the visual metaphor for their interface, representing the app's focus on keyboard accessibility. Apple's Magic Keyboard inspired the visual look, allowing for alternative icons.

For developers building extensions with Raycast, the Keyboard APIs help make actions accessible via keyboard shortcuts, with a keyboard shortcut defined by one or more modifier keys (command, control, etc.) and a single key equivalent (a character or special key).

The search results contain information about keyboard shortcut functionality and general design philosophy, but don't include specific 2026-dated content about badge styling or recent visual design updates. If you're looking for more specific information about keyboard shortcut badge styling conventions in 2026, you may want to check Raycast's API documentation or design system documentation directly.

## Summary of Search Results

The search results show that Raycast has a panel with a list of Actions where each one can be associated with a keyboard shortcut, and shortcuts allow users to use Raycast without using their mouse.

Regarding the visual display of shortcuts, Raycast displays action shortcuts to let users learn and use them to make using Raycast even faster.

For the Raycast API and UI components, the documentation covers various components like List, Grid, Detail, and Form. Raycast uses React for its user interface declaration and renders supported elements to native UI, and comes with a set of UI components that function as a design system.

However, the search results don't contain specific information about how keyboard shortcut badges appear visually in the UI, their styling properties, or specific documentation about badge appearance customization. To get more detailed information about keyboard shortcut badge appearance in Raycast, you may want to:

2. Review the Raycast GitHub repository for extension examples
3. Consult the official Raycast manual or support documentation

content.text: Now let me check other component directories to see if they follow a pattern with index files:

input.description: Check other component directories for index files

content.text: Perfect. Now I have all the information needed to provide a comprehensive PR review. Let me compile my findings. --- # PR Review: Kbd Component ## Component Overview The Kbd component is designed to render keyboard shortcuts as small inline badges (e.g., "⌘K", "↵") used in ActionPanel and ListItem accessories. ## CRITICAL ISSUES (MUST-FIX) ### 1. **Missing Index Export File** - MUST-FIX **Location:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/kbd/` **Issue:** The component lacks an `index.ts` file for clean exports. While not all components in the project use index files (action_panel, search_bar don't), the `list` component does follow this pattern. For consistency and better import ergonomics, especially since this is a reusable component that will be imported in multiple places, an index file should be added. **Fix Required:** Create `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/kbd/index.ts`: ```typescript export { Kbd } from './kbd'; export type { KbdProps } from './kbd'; ``` ### 2. **Hardcoded Background Color Instead of Token** - MUST-FIX **Location:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/kbd/kbd.scss` line 16 **Issue:** `background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06);` is hardcoded instead of using a design token. This violates the project's token-based design system and makes theme maintenance harder. **Current code:** ```scss background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06); ``` **Fix Required:** Add a new token to `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/styles/_tokens.scss`: ```scss $color-kbd-background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06); ``` Then update kbd.scss: ```scss background-color: $color-kbd-background; ``` **Rationale:** The spec states "Background slightly lighter than surface" and all other colors in the project use tokens. This ensures consistency and maintainability. ### 3. **Incorrect Token Reference** - MUST-FIX **Location:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/kbd/kbd.scss` line 17 **Issue:** Uses `$color-border` but the actual token in `_tokens.scss` is `$color-border` (line 4). Wait, checking again... the token file shows `$color-border: #3a3a3c;` on line 4. Actually, this is correct. Strike this issue. ### 4. **Missing Theme Adaptability for Active State** - MUST-FIX **Location:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/kbd/kbd.scss` **Issue:** When Kbd is used inside an active ListItem (with blue accent background), the current styling will have poor contrast. Looking at `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/list/list_item/list_item.scss` lines 32-38, accessories get themed when the item is active. **Current:** No active state handling in Kbd component. **Fix Required:** The Kbd component needs to adapt when used in active contexts. However, since Kbd is a standalone component, the parent (ListItem) should handle this. Check if ListItem properly styles nested Kbd elements when active. Actually, reviewing ListItem again - it uses `.list-item__accessory` and `.list-item__accessory-text` selectors (lines 32-38) to change colors when active. Since Kbd would be rendered inside an accessory's `icon` prop as a ReactNode, we need specific styling. **Add to kbd.scss:** ```scss .list-item--active .kbd__key { background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.15); border-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.25); color: $color-text-on-accent-secondary; } ``` This ensures keyboard shortcuts remain visible and properly styled when a list item is selected. ### 5. **Array Index as Key** - MUST-FIX **Location:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/kbd/kbd.tsx` line 11 **Issue:** Using array index as React key is an anti-pattern. **Current code:** ```tsx {keys.map((key, i) => ( ``` **Fix Required:** Since keyboard keys should be unique within a single shortcut (you wouldn't have "⌘⌘K"), use the key itself: ```tsx {keys.map((key) => ( ``` If duplicate keys are possible (e.g., for multiple modifier keys that look the same but have different meanings), then use a combination: ```tsx {keys.map((key, i) => ( ``` **Recommendation:** Use just `key={key}` unless there's a specific use case for duplicate keys in the same shortcut. ## ACCESSIBILITY ISSUES ### 6. **Incorrect ARIA Role** - MUST-FIX **Location:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/kbd/kbd.tsx` line 9 **Issue:** `role="presentation"` is semantically incorrect. This role means the element has no semantic meaning, but keyboard shortcuts DO have meaning - they convey information about available actions. **Fix Required:** Remove the role entirely or use `role="text"`: ```tsx ``` The semantic HTML `` element already provides proper meaning. The wrapper span doesn't need a role. ### 7. **Missing Accessible Label for Screen Readers** - SUGGESTION **Location:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/kbd/kbd.tsx` **Issue:** Screen reader users hear "command K" as separate characters. Symbols like "⌘" might be read as "place of interest sign" instead of "command". **Suggested Enhancement:** Add an `aria-label` with the full shortcut description: ```tsx export interface KbdProps { keys: string[]; ariaLabel?: string; } export function Kbd({ keys, ariaLabel }: KbdProps) { return ( {keys.map((key) => ( {key} ))} ); } ``` Usage: `` **Priority:** SUGGESTION (nice-to-have for accessibility, but not blocking) ## VISUAL ACCURACY CONCERNS ### 8. **Fixed Height May Clip Taller Characters** - SUGGESTION **Location:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/kbd/kbd.scss` line 14 **Issue:** `height: 18px` with `line-height: 1` might clip descenders or taller Unicode characters. **Current:** ```scss height: 18px; ``` **Suggested:** ```scss min-height: 18px; height: auto; ``` This allows the badge to grow if needed while maintaining minimum dimensions. ### 9. **Visual Comparison with Real Raycast** - SUGGESTION Based on research into Raycast's design system and the keycap visual metaphor they use (inspired by Apple's Magic Keyboard), the current implementation appears reasonable. However, I couldn't find 2026-specific documentation on exact kbd badge styling. **Recommendations for pixel-perfect accuracy:** - Test with actual Raycast screenshots side-by-side - Verify the border color - real Raycast might use a slightly lighter border than `$color-border` - Check if there's a subtle inner shadow or highlight effect on the keycaps - Confirm the exact padding - 1px vertical might be tight Sources consulted: - [Raycast Design Philosophy](https://www.raycast.com/blog/a-fresh-look-and-feel) - [Keyboard Shortcuts Documentation](https://manual.raycast.com/keyboard-shortcuts) - [Raycast API Reference](https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/keyboard) ## CODE QUALITY & CONSISTENCY ### 10. **Component Structure is Consistent** - PASS ✓ The component follows project patterns: - ✓ TypeScript with strict mode - ✓ Proper interface export - ✓ Separate `.scss` file with BEM naming - ✓ Imports tokens correctly with `@use '../../styles/tokens' as *` - ✓ No unnecessary comments - ✓ Clean, minimal implementation ### 11. **Naming Convention is Correct** - PASS ✓ File naming follows `file_name.ts` convention: - ✓ `kbd.tsx` (not `Kbd.tsx`) - ✓ `kbd.scss` (not `Kbd.scss`) ### 12. **BEM Methodology Applied Correctly** - PASS ✓ CSS class names follow BEM: - ✓ `.kbd` (block) - ✓ `.kbd__key` (element) ## MISSING EDGE CASES ### 13. **Empty Keys Array Handling** - SUGGESTION **Issue:** What happens if `keys={[]}`? The component renders an empty span. **Suggested Enhancement:** ```tsx export function Kbd({ keys }: KbdProps) { if (!keys || keys.length === 0) return null; return ( {keys.map((key) => ( {key} ))} ); } ``` ### 14. **Very Long Key Strings** - SUGGESTION **Issue:** If someone passes a long string like `keys={['Shift+Command+Option+Control+A']}`, it might overflow. **Current:** `white-space: nowrap` prevents wrapping but doesn't handle overflow. **Suggested addition to `.kbd__key`:** ```scss max-width: 60px; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; ``` Or add validation/warnings in development. ## INTEGRATION CONCERNS ### 15. **Usage Examples Missing** - SUGGESTION The component lacks usage examples.
